name: Sleep Suite Design
consequences: The design of the sleep suites will directly impact participant comfort and naturalistic behavior, affecting data quality.
options: Domestic-style bedrooms with natural light
Clinical-style rooms with controlled lighting
Hybrid environment with adjustable lighting
review: Participant comfort vs. clinical accuracy. Weakness: The options fail to account for participant preferences and individual differences in sleep behavior.

name: Tiered Sensing Model
consequences: The tiered sensing model will determine the trade-off between data resolution, participant burden, and resource allocation.
options: Enhanced nights with full PSG montage on every third night
Scheduled enhanced nights with fuller PSG montage on specific nights
Adaptive sensing based on technician-flagged events
review: Data quality vs. participant comfort. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of adaptive sensing on data consistency and reliability.

name: Participant Recruitment
consequences: The recruitment strategy will influence participant demographics, enrollment rates, and captured event yield.
options: Targeted outreach to sleep clinics and neurologist referrals
Public advertising through social media and online forums
Collaboration with patient advocacy groups
review: Participant diversity vs. recruitment efficiency. Weakness: The options fail to account for the potential biases in participant selection.

name: Event-Triage Algorithm
consequences: The development of the event-triage algorithm will impact the accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity of parasomnia episode detection.
options: Rule-based system using AASM criteria
Machine learning approach with supervised learning
Hybrid model combining rule-based and machine learning approaches
review: Algorithm accuracy vs. computational complexity. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of algorithmic bias on parasomnia episode detection.

name: Data Sharing and Governance
consequences: The data sharing policy will determine access, usage, and ownership of the collected data.
options: Open-access repository with unrestricted data sharing
Restricted access to authorized researchers with data use agreements
Private dataset with limited access for collaborative research
review: Data accessibility vs. participant privacy. Weakness: The options fail to account for the potential risks and consequences of data misuse.

name: Personnel Management
consequences: The management of personnel will impact staff retention, workload distribution, and operational efficiency.
options: Centralized team with shared responsibilities
Decentralized team with specialized roles
Hybrid model combining centralized and decentralized approaches
review: Team cohesion vs. individual expertise. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of personnel turnover on team dynamics and research continuity.

name: Budget Allocation
consequences: The budget allocation will determine resource availability, staffing levels, and operational costs.
options: Priority on equipment and infrastructure development
Focus on personnel hiring and training
Balanced approach with equal emphasis on equipment, personnel, and operations
review: Equipment vs. personnel costs. Weakness: The options fail to account for the potential risks of underinvestment in critical areas.

name: Parasomnia Phenotyping Protocol
consequences: Implementing a comprehensive phenotyping protocol for NREM parasomnias will provide a robust framework for characterizing sleep disorders, but may require significant upfront investment in staff training and equipment calibration.
options: Develop a detailed, multi-step protocol incorporating EEG, EMG, and video monitoring to capture the full spectrum of parasomnia events.
Employ machine learning algorithms to identify patterns and clusters within the collected data, streamlining phenotyping efforts.
Implement a tiered scoring system, where initial assessments are conducted by trained research technicians and later validated by board-certified sleep medicine physicians.
review: Parasomnia phenotyping protocol vs. simplified symptom tracking. Weakness: The options fail to account for potential biases in data collection and scoring.

name: Participant Retention Strategy
consequences: Implementing an effective participant retention strategy will ensure a steady supply of qualified research subjects, but may compromise the naturalistic environment required for accurate sleep disorder assessment.
options: Offer personalized support and counseling services to participants struggling with parasomnia-related anxiety or stress.
Develop a comprehensive participant engagement plan, incorporating regular updates, rewards, and incentives to maintain motivation and interest.
Implement a ' buddy system' where participants are paired with trained peer mentors who have experienced similar sleep disorders.
review: Participant retention strategy vs. strict participant selection criteria. Weakness: The options fail to account for potential conflicts of interest in the buddy system.

name: Data Sharing and Collaboration
consequences: Establishing a data sharing and collaboration framework will facilitate knowledge exchange and accelerate research progress, but may compromise participant confidentiality and intellectual property rights.
options: Develop a secure, cloud-based platform for researchers to share and access de-identified data, while maintaining strict controls on sensitive information.
Host regular workshops and conferences to foster collaboration among researchers, clinicians, and industry partners, promoting knowledge transfer and innovation.
Establish a formal data sharing agreement with the University of Bonn's Department of Epileptology, ensuring seamless integration with existing research initiatives.
review: Data sharing and collaboration vs. strict data ownership and control. Weakness: The options fail to account for potential conflicts of interest in data sharing agreements.

name: Staff Training and Development
consequences: Investing in staff training and development will ensure a skilled and motivated workforce, but may divert resources away from core research activities.
options: Develop a comprehensive training program for research technicians, focusing on parasomnia phenotyping, data collection, and equipment operation.
Provide opportunities for staff to engage in ongoing professional development, including workshops, conferences, and peer mentoring programs.
Establish a mentorship program pairing experienced staff with new hires, facilitating knowledge transfer and cultural adaptation.
review: Staff training and development vs. minimal onboarding and training. Weakness: The options fail to account for potential biases in mentorship pairings.

name: Sustainability and Scalability
consequences: Developing a sustainable and scalable research model will ensure long-term viability, but may compromise the unique characteristics of the residential research unit.
options: Implement a tiered pricing system for participant compensation, reflecting varying levels of engagement and participation.
Develop strategic partnerships with industry partners to secure funding and resources, while maintaining academic independence.
Establish a phased expansion plan, gradually increasing participant capacity and staffing levels to ensure seamless integration.
review: Sustainability and scalability vs. minimal growth and expansion. Weakness: The options fail to account for potential conflicts of interest in industry partnerships.

name: Neurological Screening Protocol
consequences: Implementing a comprehensive neurological screening protocol will ensure accurate participant selection, but may compromise the efficiency and speed of the research process.
options: Develop a multi-step screening protocol incorporating clinical interviews, collateral history, and prior sleep studies to identify potential participants.
Employ machine learning algorithms to analyze EEG data and identify potential neurological anomalies, streamlining participant selection.
Implement a tiered scoring system, where initial assessments are conducted by trained research technicians and later validated by board-certified neurologists.
review: Neurological screening protocol vs. simplified symptom tracking. Weakness: The options fail to account for potential biases in data collection and scoring.

name: Residential Unit Expansion
consequences: Expanding the residential research unit will increase participant capacity and accelerate research progress, but may compromise the unique characteristics of the facility.
options: Implement a phased expansion plan, gradually increasing participant capacity and staffing levels to ensure seamless integration.
Develop strategic partnerships with industry partners to secure funding and resources for expansion, while maintaining academic independence.
Establish a tiered pricing system for participant compensation, reflecting varying levels of engagement and participation.
review: Residential unit expansion vs. minimal growth and expansion. Weakness: The options fail to account for potential conflicts of interest in industry partnerships.

name: Participant Engagement Strategy
consequences: Developing an effective participant engagement strategy will ensure a steady supply of qualified research subjects, but may compromise the naturalistic environment required for accurate sleep disorder assessment.
options: Offer personalized support and counseling services to participants struggling with parasomnia-related anxiety or stress.
Develop a comprehensive participant engagement plan, incorporating regular updates, rewards, and incentives to maintain motivation and interest.
Implement a 'buddy system' where participants are paired with trained peer mentors who have experienced similar sleep disorders.
review: Participant engagement strategy vs. strict participant selection criteria. Weakness: The options fail to account for potential conflicts of interest in the buddy system.
